<analysis>
Let me carefully review the conversation to capture all relevant technical details.

## Session Overview

The session involved working on a patent PoC project — AI-assisted writing analysis using TipTap editor. The user (Mike Crowe, senior engineer) wanted two behaviors for template insertion:
1. Click a template → replace ALL editor text with template
2. Tab through placeholders

## Chronological Analysis

### Initial Browser Inspection
- Used Chrome MCP to read the page: `http://localhost:3000/` — "Prompt Coach PoC"
- Tab: 615982960
- Page had: textbox with ghost text "draft me an email" / "…for whom?", sidebar with Templates (2), Examples (2), Gap Checklist, Questions sections

### User Request #1: Template Click Behavior
User asked: when clicking a template, (1) replace all text with the template, (2) tab moves to each placeholder.

### Investigation
Used Serena to explore:
- `src/lib/editor/templateSlot.tsx` — `TemplateSlot` TipTap Node extension, `TemplateSlotComponent` React component
- `src/components/SuggestionSidebar.tsx` — `SuggestionSidebar` function, template click handler
- `src/components/Editor.tsx` — `Editor` function, tab handler

### Key Findings

**SuggestionSidebar.tsx onClick (original):**
- Inserted template at cursor position (not replacing all content)
- Used `let insertPos = editor.state.selection.from`
- Built transaction inserting text parts and templateSlot nodes

**Editor.tsx Tab handler (original):**
- Used DOM query `editorDom.querySelectorAll("[data-template-slot]")` — this attribute is only in `renderHTML`, NOT on the `NodeViewWrapper` DOM element
- `posAtDOM(slot, 0)` was fragile

**TemplateSlotComponent:**
- Uses `NodeViewWrapper` with `data-slot-id={node.attrs.id}` and `data-filled={filled}`
- Has `NodeViewContent` for editable content area
- Renders `⟨label⟩` with italic gray placeholder text

### Fix #1: SuggestionSidebar.tsx onClick — Replace all content
Changed from inserting at cursor to building new paragraph replacing entire document:
```javascript
onClick={() => {
  if (!editor) return;
  const parts = t.body.split(/<[^>]+>/g);
  const placeholders: string[] = [];
  let match;
  const placeholderRegex = /<([^>]+)>/g;
  while ((match = placeholderRegex.exec(t.body)) !== null) {
    placeholders.push(match[1]);
  }
  const { state, view } = editor;
  const { schema } = state;
  const contentNodes: ReturnType<typeof schema.text>[] = [];
  parts.forEach((part, idx) => {
    if (part) contentNodes.push(schema.text(part));
    if (idx < parts.length - 1 && idx < placeholders.length) {
      const slotNode = schema.nodes.templateSlot?.create({
        id: crypto.randomUUID(),
        label: placeholders[idx],
        filled: false,
      });
      if (slotNode) contentNodes.push(slotNode);
    }
  });
  const newParagraph = schema.nodes.paragraph.create(null, contentNodes);
  const tr = state.tr.replaceWith(0, state.doc.content.size, newParagraph);
  view.dispatch(tr);
  editor.commands.focus();
}}
```

### Fix #2: Editor.tsx Tab handler — Use ProseMirror doc traversal
Changed from DOM query to `editor.state.doc.descendants()`:
```javascript
const handleKeyDown = (event: KeyboardEvent) => {
  if (event.key !== "Tab") return;
  const slots: { pos: number; size: number }[] = [];
  editor.state.doc.descendants((node, pos) => {
    if (node.type.name === "templateSlot") {
      slots.push({ pos, size: node.nodeSize });
    }
  });
  if (slots.length === 0) return;
  event.preventDefault();
  const { from } = editor.state.selection;
  const next = slots.find((s) => s.pos > from) ?? slots[0];
  editor.chain().focus().setTextSelection({ from: next.pos + 1, to: next.pos + next.size - 1 }).run();
};
```

### Browser Verification #1
- Clicked template → replaced all content ✓
- Tab navigated between slots ✓
- Both working

### Bug #3: Duplicate label text
User showed screenshot where "deliverable", "topic", etc. appeared as plain text AFTER each slot bracket.

**Root cause:** `t.body.split(placeholderRegex)` where `placeholderRegex = /<([^>]+)>/g` — the **capturing group** causes JavaScript's `split()` to include captured groups in the result array.

`"Write <a> about <b>".split(/<([^>]+)>/g)` → `["Write ", "a", " about ", "b", ""]`

So `parts[1]` = "a", `parts[3]` = "b" — these got inserted as plain text nodes after each slot.

**Fix:** Changed split regex to non-capturing: `t.body.split(/<[^>]+>/g)`

Before the fix:
```javascript
const placeholderRegex = /<([^>]+)>/g;
const parts = t.body.split(placeholderRegex);
```

After the fix:
```javascript
const parts = t.body.split(/<[^>]+>/g);
const placeholders: string[] = [];
let match;
const placeholderRegex = /<([^>]+)>/g;
while ((match = placeholderRegex.exec(t.body)) !== null) {
  placeholders.push(match[1]);
}
```

### Browser Verification #2
After fix, editor correctly shows: `Write ⟨  ⟩ about ⟨  ⟩ for ⟨  ⟩ in ⟨  ⟩.` — no stray label text ✓

### Bug Reports #4 and #5 (pending)
User reported two new issues:
1. **Analysis fires immediately on template insert** → LLM returns suggestions that conflict with template (e.g. template: "write ⟨placeholder⟩", suggestion: "write to ⟨placeholder⟩")
2. **Accept suggestion replaces with literal text** → When suggestion `replacement` contains `<placeholder>` syntax, `insertContent(suggestion.replacement)` inserts literal `<placeholder>` text instead of creating slot nodes

Assistant started to investigate but got blocked by `code-nav-gate` when trying to `Read` Editor.tsx at line 36 without an MCP call to unlock. No fixes were applied before context reset.

## Current State of Files

### SuggestionSidebar.tsx (modified, correct)
Current onClick handler (lines ~95-128):
```javascript
onClick={() => {
  if (!editor) return;
  const parts = t.body.split(/<[^>]+>/g);
  const placeholders: string[] = [];
  let match;
  const placeholderRegex = /<([^>]+)>/g;
  while ((match = placeholderRegex.exec(t.body)) !== null) {
    placeholders.push(match[1]);
  }
  const { state, view } = editor;
  const { schema } = state;
  const contentNodes: ReturnType<typeof schema.text>[] = [];
  parts.forEach((part, idx) => {
    if (part) contentNodes.push(schema.text(part));
    if (idx < parts.length - 1 && idx < placeholders.length) {
      const slotNode = schema.nodes.templateSlot?.create({
        id: crypto.randomUUID(),
        label: placeholders[idx],
        filled: false,
      });
      if (slotNode) contentNodes.push(slotNode);
    }
  });
  const newParagraph = schema.nodes.paragraph.create(null, contentNodes);
  const tr = state.tr.replaceWith(0, state.doc.content.size, newParagraph);
  view.dispatch(tr);
  editor.commands.focus();
}}
```

`handleAccept` (current, needs fix):
```javascript
function handleAccept(suggestion: Suggestion) {
  if (!editor) return;
  const from = suggestion.start + 1;
  const to = suggestion.end + 1;
  editor.chain().focus()
    .setTextSelection({ from, to })
    .insertContent(suggestion.replacement)
    .run();
  handleDismiss(suggestion.id);
}
```

### Editor.tsx (modified, correct)
Tab handler uses doc traversal. `onUpdate` still calls `debouncedAnalyze` unconditionally (this is the bug to fix).

## Pending Fixes

**Bug 1 fix plan:**
In `Editor.tsx` `onUpdate`, check for templateSlot nodes and skip `debouncedAnalyze`:
```javascript
let hasSlots = false;
ed.state.doc.descendants((node) => {
  if (node.type.name === 'templateSlot') hasSlots = true;
});
if (!hasSlots) {
  debouncedAnalyze(text);
}
```

**Bug 2 fix plan:**
In `SuggestionSidebar.tsx` `handleAccept`, detect `<placeholder>` in replacement and build slot nodes:
```javascript
function handleAccept(suggestion: Suggestion) {
  if (!editor) return;
  const hasPlaceholders = /<[^>]+>/.test(suggestion.replacement);
  if (hasPlaceholders) {
    // parse and build slot nodes, replace range
  } else {
    // existing plain text behavior
  }
  handleDismiss(suggestion.id);
}
```

## Nav Gate
Always call `mcp__serena__find_symbol` or CBM MCP before Read/Grep on source files.
</analysis>

<summary>
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   User (Mike Crowe) wanted template interaction in TipTap editor to work correctly:
   - Click template in sidebar → replaces ALL editor text (not insert at cursor)
   - Tab key moves between `<placeholder>` slots after template insertion
   Then after testing, reported two new bugs:
   - Analysis fires immediately on template insert, returning suggestions that conflict with template structure (e.g. template says "write ⟨placeholder⟩", suggestion says "write to ⟨placeholder⟩")
   - Accepting a suggestion whose `replacement` contains `<placeholder>` syntax inserts it as literal `<>` text, not as functional slot nodes

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - TipTap v2 / ProseMirror — `NodeView`, `NodeViewWrapper`, `NodeViewContent`, `ReactNodeViewRenderer`
   - `templateSlot` — custom inline TipTap Node extension with `atom: false`, renders `⟨label⟩` brackets
   - ProseMirror transactions — `tr.replaceWith(from, to, content)` for document replacement
   - `editor.state.doc.descendants()` — traverses ProseMirror document tree to find nodes by type
   - `editor.state.tr` — building raw transactions for complex insertions
   - JavaScript `String.split()` with capturing groups includes captured groups in result array (root cause of duplicate label bug)
   - `renderHTML` attributes (e.g. `data-template-slot`) are NOT applied to `NodeViewWrapper` DOM — only in serialization
   - Debounced analysis: `onUpdate` → `debouncedAnalyze(text)` — fires on every doc change including template insertion

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - `src/components/SuggestionSidebar.tsx`
     - Contains template click handler and `handleAccept` function
     - Template onClick was fixed: now uses `state.tr.replaceWith(0, state.doc.content.size, newParagraph)` to replace entire document
     - Split regex fixed: `t.body.split(/<[^>]+>/g)` (non-capturing) instead of `split(/<([^>]+)>/g)` (capturing, which caused duplicate label text)
     - `handleAccept` still needs fix for `<placeholder>` in replacement text
     - Current onClick (working):
       ```javascript
       const parts = t.body.split(/<[^>]+>/g);
       const placeholders: string[] = [];
       let match;
       const placeholderRegex = /<([^>]+)>/g;
       while ((match = placeholderRegex.exec(t.body)) !== null) {
         placeholders.push(match[1]);
       }
       const { state, view } = editor;
       const { schema } = state;
       const contentNodes: ReturnType<typeof schema.text>[] = [];
       parts.forEach((part, idx) => {
         if (part) contentNodes.push(schema.text(part));
         if (idx < parts.length - 1 && idx < placeholders.length) {
           const slotNode = schema.nodes.templateSlot?.create({
             id: crypto.randomUUID(), label: placeholders[idx], filled: false,
           });
           if (slotNode) contentNodes.push(slotNode);
         }
       });
       const newParagraph = schema.nodes.paragraph.create(null, contentNodes);
       const tr = state.tr.replaceWith(0, state.doc.content.size, newParagraph);
       view.dispatch(tr);
       editor.commands.focus();
       ```
     - Current `handleAccept` (needs fix):
       ```javascript
       function handleAccept(suggestion: Suggestion) {
         if (!editor) return;
         const from = suggestion.start + 1;
         const to = suggestion.end + 1;
         editor.chain().focus()
           .setTextSelection({ from, to })
           .insertContent(suggestion.replacement)
           .run();
         handleDismiss(suggestion.id);
       }
       ```

   - `src/components/Editor.tsx`
     - Tab handler fixed: uses `editor.state.doc.descendants()` to find `templateSlot` nodes by type, selects content range `{ from: next.pos + 1, to: next.pos + next.size - 1 }`
     - `onUpdate` still calls `debouncedAnalyze(text)` unconditionally — needs fix to skip when slots present
     - Current tab handler (working):
       ```javascript
       const handleKeyDown = (event: KeyboardEvent) => {
         if (event.key !== "Tab") return;
         const slots: { pos: number; size: number }[] = [];
         editor.state.doc.descendants((node, pos) => {
           if (node.type.name === "templateSlot") {
             slots.push({ pos, size: node.nodeSize });
           }
         });
         if (slots.length === 0) return;
         event.preventDefault();
         const { from } = editor.state.selection;
         const next = slots.find((s) => s.pos > from) ?? slots[0];
         editor.chain().focus().setTextSelection({ from: next.pos + 1, to: next.pos + next.size - 1 }).run();
       };
       ```

   - `src/lib/editor/templateSlot.tsx`
     - `TemplateSlotComponent`: `NodeViewWrapper` with `data-slot-id` and `data-filled` attrs; `NodeViewContent` for editable area; brackets `⟨` `⟩` with `contentEditable={false}`
     - `TemplateSlot` Node: `inline: true`, `atom: false`, no `content` spec, `addKeyboardShortcuts` returns `Tab: () => false`
     - `renderHTML` sets `data-template-slot=""` — this is NOT on the NodeViewWrapper DOM element (only for serialization)

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **Tab handler used `[data-template-slot]` DOM query**: `data-template-slot` is set by `renderHTML` not by `NodeViewWrapper`. Fixed by switching to `editor.state.doc.descendants()` traversal.
   - **Template click inserted at cursor instead of replacing all content**: Used `editor.state.selection.from` as insert position. Fixed with `state.tr.replaceWith(0, state.doc.content.size, newParagraph)`.
   - **Duplicate label text after slot** (user caught this): `t.body.split(/<([^>]+)>/g)` with capturing group makes JS include captured groups in split result — `parts[1]`="deliverable", `parts[3]`="topic" etc. were inserted as plain text nodes after each slot. Fixed by switching to non-capturing split `/<[^>]+>/g`.
   - **code-nav-gate blocked Read without MCP unlock**: Must call `mcp__serena__find_symbol` or CBM before Read/Grep on source files. Nav gate unlocks for 120s after MCP call.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Template insert + tab navigation: all three bugs (wrong insert position, broken tab DOM query, duplicate label text) fixed and verified in browser
   - Two new bugs identified (analysis fires on template insert; accept inserts placeholder as literal text) but not yet fixed — context reset mid-investigation

6. All user messages:
   - "Using your Chrome MCP, can you see what's currently on the Chrome page?"
   - "I just asked you to do just that. why ask me again?" (correcting assistant for asking redundant question)
   - "when i click on one of the templates, it should: 1. replace all the text with the template 2. move to each placeholder sections as I press tab"
   - "so, I started to fill in but look at what it looks like now. Take a screenshot and digest" (reporting duplicate label text bug)
   - "So if you look at the suggestions offered, I accepted the basic suggestions coming from the template. So there's a disconnect between the template and what the suggestions coming back are. The template should not immediately come up as a suggestion. For example, the template said write and then had an immediate placeholder, but the suggestion says write to and then the placeholder. next if I accept one of the suggestions those are technically placeholders where I need to complete but they get replaced as if they were getting inserted"

7. Pending Tasks:
   - **Bug 1**: Suppress analysis when template slots are present in the document — prevent `debouncedAnalyze` from firing in `Editor.tsx` `onUpdate` when `templateSlot` nodes exist
   - **Bug 2**: Fix `handleAccept` in `SuggestionSidebar.tsx` — when `suggestion.replacement` contains `<placeholder>` syntax, build slot nodes instead of calling `insertContent()` with raw text

8. Current Work:
   Working on the two bugs reported by the user after template testing:
   - Bug 1: Analysis fires on template insert → `Editor.tsx` `onUpdate` calls `debouncedAnalyze` unconditionally. Fix: check `ed.state.doc.descendants()` for `templateSlot` nodes; skip debounce if any found.
   - Bug 2: `handleAccept` in `SuggestionSidebar.tsx` calls `editor.chain().insertContent(suggestion.replacement)` which treats `<placeholder>` as raw text. Fix: detect `/<[^>]+>/` in replacement, parse into text + slot nodes, dispatch a `tr.replaceWith` transaction.
   
   Assistant was blocked by `code-nav-gate` when attempting `Read` on `Editor.tsx` at line 36. No code changes applied yet for these two bugs.

9. Optional Next Step:
   The user said: "the template should not immediately come up as a suggestion... if I accept one of the suggestions those are technically placeholders where I need to complete but they get replaced as if they were getting inserted"

   Next: Call `mcp__serena__find_symbol` for `"onUpdate"` or `"Editor"` in `Editor.tsx` to unlock nav gate, then:
   
   **Fix Bug 1** — add to `onUpdate` in `Editor.tsx` before `debouncedAnalyze(text)`:
   ```javascript
   let hasSlots = false;
   ed.state.doc.descendants((node) => {
     if (node.type.name === 'templateSlot') hasSlots = true;
   });
   if (!hasSlots) {
     debouncedAnalyze(text);
   }
   ```

   **Fix Bug 2** — modify `handleAccept` in `SuggestionSidebar.tsx`:
   ```javascript
   function handleAccept(suggestion: Suggestion) {
     if (!editor) return;
     const hasPlaceholders = /<[^>]+>/.test(suggestion.replacement);
     if (hasPlaceholders) {
       const parts = suggestion.replacement.split(/<[^>]+>/g);
       const placeholders: string[] = [];
       let m;
       const re = /<([^>]+)>/g;
       while ((m = re.exec(suggestion.replacement)) !== null) placeholders.push(m[1]);
       const { state, view } = editor;
       const { schema } = state;
       const contentNodes: ReturnType<typeof schema.text>[] = [];
       parts.forEach((part, idx) => {
         if (part) contentNodes.push(schema.text(part));
         if (idx < parts.length - 1) {
           const slotNode = schema.nodes.templateSlot?.create({
             id: crypto.randomUUID(), label: placeholders[idx], filled: false,
           });
           if (slotNode) contentNodes.push(slotNode);
         }
       });
       const newParagraph = schema.nodes.paragraph.create(null, contentNodes);
       const tr = state.tr.replaceWith(0, state.doc.content.size, newParagraph);
       view.dispatch(tr);
       editor.commands.focus();
     } else {
       const from = suggestion.start + 1;
       const to = suggestion.end + 1;
       editor.chain().focus().setTextSelection({ from, to }).insertContent(suggestion.replacement).run();
     }
     handleDismiss(suggestion.id);
   }
   ```
</summary>
